title: origanum hirtum var. latifolium
intro: Origanum hirtum var. latifolium, also known as sweet marjoram, is a perennial herb in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for centuries for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant's leaves contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Sweet marjoram can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or infused oil, and is also used as a flavoring agent in cooking and traditional medicine.
